_meta.notes: Numbers reflect base game values in v1.0.x. Growth times and yields can be sped up by Well-being, Beehives (Folktails crops), and other modifiers; base values are given here. Fields marked _uncertain are best-estimate and should be verified in-game.

trees.grows_on: dry land; tolerant, low water/moisture need; fastest starter tree
trees.notes: Fastest-maturing tree. Best default choice for early/rapid wood since 1 log every 7 days beats Pine's ramp-up when you need volume fast. Low logs-per-tree but high logs-per-day for early game.

trees.grows_on: dry land; low water/moisture need, drought-tolerant
trees.notes: Common default forester tree. Also yields Pine Resin (a material for advanced recipes) when a Gatherer Flag covers it. 2 logs per tree; good balance of speed and wood.
Highest logs-per-tree (8) and best long-run logs-per-day efficiency (~0.27), but very slow to mature. Best for established mid/late-game lumber plantations, not early game.
Folktails-only. Dual purpose: logs when felled, plus Maple Syrup harvested by a Gatherer Flag while it stands (continuous food source without cutting the tree). Slow growth.
Folktails-only. Standing trees yield Chestnuts (food) via Gatherer Flag; can also be felled for 4 logs. A renewable food + wood tree. Slower than berries to first food but productive once mature.

trees.grows_on: wet/flooded terrain; must be planted in shallow water (waterway tiles)
trees.notes: Iron Teeth-only. Grows on/over water and yields Mangrove Fruit (food) plus logs. Key to Iron Teeth waterway-based food economy. Relatively fast for a dual-purpose tree.

crops.water_need: irrigated land (needs soil moisture from water source); not aquatic
crops.notes: Earliest and fastest-growing farmed FOOD. Default first crop for Folktails. Can be eaten raw or cooked. Beehive reduces growth to ~2.8 days.
Raw Potato is a processing input; Grilled Potato (Grill/Gristmill chain) is high-nutrition food. Beehive ~4.2 days.
Milled into Flour then baked into Bread (high-nutrition food). Beehive ~7.0 days.

crops.food_or_material: material (Sunflower Seeds; pressed for oil/food chain)
crops.notes: Yields 2 Sunflower Seeds; input to oil/food production. Beehive ~3.5 days.
Grown on water via Aquatic Farmhouse. Yields Cattail Roots (processing input). Beehive ~5.6 days.
Water crop for Folktails aquatic farming. Slow. Beehive ~8.4 days.
Iron Teeth's fastest-growing farmed FOOD and earliest reliable food crop for that faction. Directly edible.
Iron Teeth processing crop; feeds into food chains.
Iron Teeth crop; input to food processing.
Yields 3 Canola Seeds; oil/food chain input.
Slow Iron Teeth crop; higher yield per harvest.

gatherables.name: Blueberry Bush
gatherables.notes: Wild bushes also spawn naturally on maps at start, so Berries are the EARLIEST food with zero infrastructure (just place a Gatherer Flag over existing wild bushes). Planted bushes are slow: 12 days to mature + 12 days to bear berries. Berries are 'unsatisfying'/low-variety food but keep beavers alive early.

gatherables.name: Dandelion Bush
gatherables.notes: NOT food. Dandelions are used only to make Medicine at the Healer. Regrows on its own (~3 days) without replanting after harvest.

gatherables.name: Coffee Bush
gatherables.notes: NOT food. Coffee Beans are a material used for Coffee production (Well-being/stimulant good). ~9 days to mature then ~3 days per harvest.

beaver_food_consumption_per_day.notes: Adult beaver eats ~2.5-3.0 food units/day; ~2.67 average. Higher-nutrition processed foods (Bread, Grilled Potato) sate more per unit, so beavers eat fewer total units on a good diet. Rule of thumb for planning: budget ~3 food/beaver/day raw.

beaver_water_consumption_per_day.notes: Thirst depletes ~70%/day and each Water unit restores ~33%, so ~70/33 = ~2.12 Water/beaver/day. Budget ~2.2 water/beaver/day.

food_economy.food_variety_wellbeing_notes: Well-being (formerly Bonus/mood) scales work speed and movement speed for adults, and improves kit growth speed and adult lifespan. Food VARIETY is a major Well-being contributor: eating several distinct food types raises Well-being. In v1.0 individual foods no longer give per-food stat bonuses; instead hungry beavers pick their next meal based on nutrition needs, and a varied larder maximizes Well-being. Practical target: aim for 3+ distinct foods available as soon as possible (e.g. Berries + Carrots + a cooked food).

starter_food_options.option: Wild Berries (Blueberry Bushes) via Gatherer Flag
starter_food_options.why: Zero build cost; harvest bushes already on the map from day 1. The earliest food available.
starter_food_options.sustainability: Low-moderate: finite wild bushes; plant more via Forester for renewal but planted bushes are slow (24 days to first berries).

starter_food_options.why: Fastest farmed food at 4-day growth, yield 3; the earliest SUSTAINABLE food you fully control. Only needs a Farmhouse + irrigated field.
starter_food_options.sustainability: High: renewable, fast cycle, controllable scale. The backbone starter food for Folktails.

starter_food_options.why: Iron Teeth equivalent of Carrot: 3-day growth, yield 2, directly edible. Earliest sustainable IT food.
starter_food_options.sustainability: High: fast and renewable for Iron Teeth.

starter_food_options.option: Chestnuts / Maple Syrup / Mangrove Fruit (dual-purpose trees)
starter_food_options.why: Renewable tree food harvested by Gatherer Flag while trees stand.
starter_food_options.sustainability: Moderate: slow to establish (23-28 day trees for Folktails; 10-day Mangrove for IT) but very stable once mature; adds food variety for Well-being.
